title: The Sixth Sunday of Easter
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
## Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
## May 9, 2021 | Preacher: Broderick Greer

---

LITURGICAL CONTEXT

The Sixth Sunday of Easter falls within the Easter Season, specifically the week before Ascension Day (May 30, 2021). Under the Revised Common Lectionary, the readings would have included Acts 17:22-31 (Paul's address at the Areopagus), 1 Peter 3:15-18 (be ready to give a reason for your hope), and John 14:15-21 (Jesus on love and obedience). This Sunday sits in the unique liturgical space between Easter's resurrection joy and Ascension's preparation for Christ's departure, thematically emphasizing the Holy Spirit's coming role and Christian witness.

---

EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was navigating pandemic-related reopening policies, with many dioceses including Colorado still operating under modified worship guidelines. The national church was also in the afterglow of General Convention 2021 (not yet convened in July) anticipation, though no major denominational crises or bishop elections were active in early May. The Diocese of Colorado under Bishop Kimberly Lucas was focused on pandemic recovery and racial justice initiatives that had intensified since 2020.

---

DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver was experiencing unusually warm spring weather in early May (highs in the 70s-80s) after a relatively dry winter. Mayor Michael Hancock's administration was managing post-pandemic reopening of city services and businesses, with Colorado moving toward full reopening by May 25. The Capitol Hill neighborhood, home to Saint John's Cathedral, was beginning to show signs of pandemic-era economic strain, with some storefronts remaining vacant but foot traffic gradually increasing.

---

NATIONAL NEWS (US)

The Biden administration was ramping down federal COVID-19 vaccination sites, with supply outpacing demand as vaccination rates plateaued around 40% of the population. The House voted 420-9 on May 6 to establish a commission investigating the January 6 Capitol attack, a sharply partisan vote that dominated cable news. The Derek Chauvin trial had concluded with conviction on May 3, but Minneapolis was still processing the aftermath and ongoing police reform debates.

---

INTERNATIONAL NEWS

India was experiencing a catastrophic COVID-19 surge with daily cases exceeding 400,000 and hospitals overwhelmed; this humanitarian crisis dominated international headlines and prompted global aid discussions. Brazil was also reporting record deaths from COVID-19 under Jair Bolsonaro's controversial handling of the pandemic. The Israeli-Palestinian conflict had recently escalated with the May 2021 conflict in Gaza (May 10-21), so tensions were heightening just as this sermon was preached.

---

CULTURAL MOMENT

America was in a cautiously optimistic but fragmented mood: vaccinated individuals were beginning to travel and gather freely, but vaccination hesitancy and conspiracy theories were deeply entrenched, creating a sharp cultural divide. The country remained polarized over election integrity and January 6 accountability. In Denver, sports fans were focused on the Nuggets' disappointing playoff positioning and the Rockies' mediocre season start, while Marvel's "Loki" had just premiered on Disney+ (June 9, so very soon), dominating pop culture anticipation.
